title: [homebrew] macos `brew services start podman` failure
body:
### Issue Description

On MacOS, Podman installed via Homebrew, there is a launchctl-based service manager accessed via `brew services ...` which can be used to auto-start daemons, e.g. It's extremely convenient, and Podman has some hooks into this brew feature, but it's missing some support for MacOS users.

```
❯ brew services start podman
[snip]
Error: Invalid usage: Formula `podman` has not implemented #plist, #service or provided a locatable service file.
```

My system:

```
❯ brew info podman
==> podman ✔: stable 5.8.2 (bottled), HEAD
Tool for managing OCI containers and pods
https://podman.io/
Installed (on request)
/opt/homebrew/Cellar/podman/5.8.2 (217 files, 92.4MB) *
  Poured from bottle using the formulae.brew.sh API on 2026-04-15 at 10:55:57
From: https://github.com/Homebrew/homebrew-core/blob/HEAD/Formula/p/podman.rb
License: Apache-2.0 AND GPL-3.0-or-later
==> Dependencies
Dependents: 1
==> Requirements
Required: macOS >= 13 (or Linux) ✔
==> Options
--HEAD
	Install HEAD version
==> Caveats
In order to run containers locally, podman depends on a Linux kernel.
One can be started manually using `podman machine` from this package.
To start a podman VM automatically at login, also install the cask
"podman-desktop".
==> Downloading https://formulae.brew.sh/api/formula/podman.json
==> Analytics

install-on-request: 24,261 (30 days), 74,234 (90 days), 235,015 (365 days)
build-error: 31 (30 days)
```

Tentative Podman integration with Brew on MacOS:

```
❯ brew services list
Name             Status   User File
atuin            error  1 jlf  ~/Library/LaunchAgents/homebrew.mxcl.atuin.plist
podman           none
```

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. brew services start podman


### Describe the results you received

`Error: Invalid usage: Formula `podman` has not implemented #plist, #service or provided a locatable service file.`


### Describe the results you expected

I expect Podman Machine to be launched in the background when launched via `brew services start` integration.

### podman info output

```yaml
If you are unable to run podman info for any reason, please provide the podman version, operating system and its version and the architecture you are running.
```

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

Monkey-See-Monkey-Do, here's Atuin's launcher which is automatically created via native Homebrew functionality: https://github.com/Homebrew/homebrew-core/blob/HEAD/Formula/a/atuin.rb#L28-L33



THANK YOU PODMAN :) Really useful set of technologies, thank you very much.

comments.body: issues with brew should be reported again the brew repo not upstream, we do not control how brew packages podman.
